message.content:
Find the complete match results for the FIRST 10 ROUNDS (matchdays) of the 1974/75 English Football League First Division season (season started 17 August 1974).

Primary source to try: https://www.worldfootball.net/all_matches/eng-premier-league-1974-1975/ (it lists every match grouped by "1. Round" ... "42. Round" with dates and scores). Also try https://www.worldfootball.net/schedule/eng-premier-league-1974-1975-spieltag/1/ through .../10/ if the all_matches page is unwieldy. Fallbacks: 11v11.com match archive, statto mirrors, rsssf.

There were 22 teams, so 11 matches per round = 110 matches over 10 rounds.

Deliverables — write BOTH to disk:
1. /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-jramirezolmos-Documents-personal-span-league-table/ed232752-4a2f-47aa-9632-c8175fcab651/scratchpad/matches.csv with header `round,date,home_team,away_team,home_goals,away_goals` and one row per match, dates as YYYY-MM-DD, rounds 1..10. Use full official club names (e.g. "Tottenham Hotspur", "Wolverhampton Wanderers", "Queens Park Rangers", "Stoke City").
2. /private/tmp/.../scratchpad/sources.md listing the exact URLs you used.

Verification you MUST perform before reporting done:
- Exactly 110 matches, 11 per round.
- Each of the 22 teams appears exactly once per round (no team twice in a round, none missing).
- The 22 teams must be the actual 1974/75 First Division clubs — cross-check the club list against the 1974-75 Football League First Division Wikipedia page.
- Sanity-check the aggregate: compute the table after 10 rounds (2 pts a win, 1 a draw) and report the top 5 teams with P/W/D/L/GF/GA/Pts in your final message, so it can be sanity-checked against contemporary reports (Ipswich Town, Everton, Manchester City, Liverpool, Middlesbrough, Stoke were around the top early that season).

Report in your final message: the file paths, the source URLs, whether all verification checks passed, the list of 22 clubs, and the top-5 table. If any match score is uncertain or you had to guess, say so explicitly — do NOT fabricate scores.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- telegram:access: Manage Telegram channel access — approve pairings, edit allowlists, set DM/group policy. Use when the user asks to pair, approve someone, check who's allowed, or change policy for the Telegram channel.
- telegram:configure: Set up the Telegram channel — save the bot token and review access policy. Use when the user pastes a Telegram bot token, asks to configure Telegram, asks "how do I set this up" or "who can reach me," or wants to check channel status.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- claude-in-chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension). - When the user wants to interact with web pages, automate browser tasks, capture screenshots, read console logs, or perform any browser-based actions. Always invoke BEFORE attempting to use any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll fetch the match data from worldfootball.net.

input.prompt: List every match in rounds 1 through 10 ("1. Round" .. "10. Round"). For each: round number, date, home team, away team, home goals, away goals. Output as CSV lines.

content.content:
The server returned HTTP 403 Forbidden.

The response body was not retrieved. If this URL requires authentication, use an authenticated tool (e.g. `gh` for GitHub, or an MCP-provided fetch tool) instead of WebFetch.

cd /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-jramirezolmos-Documents-personal-span-league-table/ed232752-4a2f-47aa-9632-c8175fcab651/scratchpad && curl -sL "https://r.jina.ai/https://www.worldfootball.net/all_matches/eng-premier-league-1974-1975/" -o wf2.txt -w "%{http_code}\n" && wc -l wf2.txt && head -50 wf2.txt

content.text: All 10 rounds retrieved. Now I'll parse into CSV and verify.

input.command:
cd /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-jramirezolmos-Documents-personal-span-league-table/ed232752-4a2f-47aa-9632-c8175fcab651/scratchpad && cat > parse.py <<'EOF'
import re, csv, collections
lines=[l.strip() for l in open('wf2.txt')]
start=lines.index('Matchday 1')
end=lines.index('Matchday 11')
seg=lines[start:end]
NAME={'Middlesbrough FC':'Middlesbrough','Liverpool FC':'Liverpool','Arsenal FC':'Arsenal',
'Everton FC':'Everton','Chelsea FC':'Chelsea','Burnley FC':'Burnley'}
rows=[];rnd=None;date=None;i=0
while i<len(seg):
    l=seg[i]
    m=re.fullmatch(r'Matchday (\d+)',l)
    if m: rnd=int(m.group(1)); i+=1; continue
    m=re.fullmatch(r'(\d{2})\.(\d{2})\.(\d{4})',l)
    if m: date=f"{m.group(3)}-{m.group(2)}-{m.group(1)}"; i+=1; continue
    sc=re.fullmatch(r'(\d+):(\d+)',seg[i+1])
    assert sc,(l,seg[i+1])
    home=NAME.get(l,l); away=NAME.get(seg[i+2],seg[i+2])
    rows.append([rnd,date,home,away,int(sc.group(1)),int(sc.group(2))])
    i+=3
with open('matches.csv','w',newline='') as f:
    w=csv.writer(f); w.writerow(['round','date','home_team','away_team','home_goals','away_goals']); w.writerows(rows)
print('total',len(rows))
teams=set()
for r in range(1,11):
    rr=[x for x in rows if x[0]==r]
    ts=[x[2] for x in rr]+[x[3] for x in rr]
    teams|=set(ts)
    print(r,len(rr),'dupes' if len(set(ts))!=22 else 'ok22')
print('teams',len(teams));print(sorted(teams))
T=collections.defaultdict(lambda:[0]*7)
for _,_,h,a,hg,ag in rows:
    for t,gf,ga in ((h,hg,ag),(a,ag,hg)):
        s=T[t];s[0]+=1;s[4]+=gf;s[5]+=ga
        if gf>ga: s[1]+=1;s[6]+=2
        elif gf==ga: s[2]+=1;s[6]+=1
        else: s[3]+=1
tab=sorted(T.items(),key=lambda kv:(-kv[1][6],-(kv[1][4]/kv[1][5] if kv[1][5] else 999)))
print(f"{'Team':24}P  W  D  L GF GA Pts  GAvg")
for t,s in tab:
    print(f"{t:24}{s[0]:2} {s[1]:2} {s[2]:2} {s[3]:2} {s[4]:2} {s[5]:2} {s[6]:3}  {s[4]/s[5]:.3f}")
EOF
python3 parse.py

Based on the final league table provided, here are the 22 clubs that competed in the First Division during the 1974-75 season:

1. Derby County
2. Liverpool
3. Ipswich Town
4. Everton
5. Stoke City
6. Sheffield United
7. Middlesbrough
8. Manchester City
9. Leeds United

18. Leicester City
19. Tottenham Hotspur

The table shows that "Derby County finally winning the title in their first full season under the management of Dave Mackay," while the bottom three teams (Luton Town, Chelsea, and Carlisle United) were relegated to the Second Division.

input.content:
# Sources — 1974/75 English First Division, rounds 1–10

## Match results (dates, fixtures, scores)
- https://www.worldfootball.net/all_matches/eng-premier-league-1974-1975/
  (full season schedule grouped by "Matchday 1" … "Matchday 42", with dates and scores.
  The site returns HTTP 403 to plain clients; fetched via the text-extraction proxy
  https://r.jina.ai/https://www.worldfootball.net/all_matches/eng-premier-league-1974-1975/ )

## Club list cross-check (22 First Division clubs)
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1974%E2%80%9375_Football_League
  (final First Division table; the 22 clubs match the 22 clubs appearing in the match data exactly)

## Notes
- Round = worldfootball.net's "Matchday" (fixture round), not calendar week.
- Three fixtures nominally in Round 9 were postponed and actually played much later; the
  `date` column records the date the match was actually played:
  - 1974-12-04 Leeds United 2–1 Tottenham Hotspur
  - 1974-12-10 Middlesbrough 3–0 Leicester City
  - 1975-04-23 Newcastle United 3–1 Arsenal
  Similar postponements exist in later rounds (e.g. Round 13). This is exactly the
  "10 matchdays played vs. 10th calendar week" ambiguity: a table built from rounds 1–10
  is NOT identical to the table as it stood in the press in late September 1974.

content.text:
Done.

## Files
- `/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-jramirezolmos-Documents-personal-span-league-table/ed232752-4a2f-47aa-9632-c8175fcab651/scratchpad/matches.csv` — 110 rows + header (`round,date,home_team,away_team,home_goals,away_goals`)
- `/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-jramirezolmos-Documents-personal-span-league-table/ed232752-4a2f-47aa-9632-c8175fcab651/scratchpad/sources.md`

## Sources
- `https://www.worldfootball.net/all_matches/eng-premier-league-1974-1975/` — the site 403s plain clients, so it was fetched through the text proxy `https://r.jina.ai/https://www.worldfootball.net/all_matches/eng-premier-league-1974-1975/`. Rounds are worldfootball's "Matchday 1"…"Matchday 10".
- `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1974–75_Football_League` — club-list cross-check.

## Verification — all checks passed
- Exactly 110 matches; exactly 11 in each of rounds 1–10.
- Each of the 22 teams appears exactly once per round in all 10 rounds (no duplicates, none missing).
- The 22 clubs in the data are an exact set-match with the 22 clubs in Wikipedia's final 1974–75 First Division table.

No scores were guessed or inferred — every row comes verbatim from the worldfootball schedule.

## The 22 clubs
Arsenal, Birmingham City, Burnley, Carlisle United, Chelsea, Coventry City, Derby County, Everton, Ipswich Town, Leeds United, Leicester City, Liverpool, Luton Town, Manchester City, Middlesbrough, Newcastle United, Queens Park Rangers, Sheffield United, Stoke City, Tottenham Hotspur, West Ham United, Wolverhampton Wanderers.

## Table after 10 rounds (2 pts a win, 1 a draw; ties shown by goal average)
```
Team                 P  W  D  L  GF GA Pts  GAvg
Ipswich Town        10  8  0  2  18  6   16  3.000
Newcastle United    10  6  2  2  19 14   14  1.357
Manchester City     10  6  2  2  14 11   14  1.273
Middlesbrough       10  5  3  2  15  7   13  2.143
Liverpool           10  6  1  3  17  8   13  2.125
Everton             10  4  5  1  14 11   13  1.273
Sheffield United    10  5  3  2  14 14   13  1.000
```
(Everton and Sheffield United are also on 13, shown for context.) This lines up with contemporary reports: Ipswich clear at the top, with Everton, Manchester City, Liverpool, Middlesbrough and Stoke (11 pts, 9th) all in the leading group.

## One caveat worth carrying into the README
Three "Round 9" fixtures were postponed and actually played much later — Leeds 2–1 Tottenham (1974-12-04), Middlesbrough 3–0 Leicester (1974-12-10), Newcastle 3–1 Arsenal (1975-04-23) — and the `date` column records the real date played. So a table built from rounds 1–10 is a "10 matchdays completed" table, not the table as printed in the papers in late September 1974. That is precisely the ambiguity CLAUDE.md asks you to name and justify; the data supports either reading (filter by `round <= 10`, or filter by `date <= 1974-09-28`, which yields 107 matches and unequal games played).
